page: Amazon Web Services m7a.2xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The CPU includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the m7a.2xlarge exhibits strong single-core performance, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng single-core CPU testing and Geekbench single-core scores. It also achieves top-tier status in Geekbench text processing. Multi-core performance, including stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core metrics, falls within the average range. Memory bandwidth tests, such as uncached read and write operations, also align with average tier performance. For specialized workloads, the processor supports AVX-512 vector instructions and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI, yielding average results in extended instruction and encryption benchmarks.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m7a.2xlarge offers a balanced resource profile suitable for general-purpose workloads that require modern DDR5 memory speeds and dedicated CPU allocation without the overhead of local storage. Given its strong single-core capabilities and average multi-core throughput, this instance is well-suited for applications such as web serving, text processing, software compilation, and moderate database operations. It can also handle lightweight machine learning inference tasks, as demonstrated by its average to strong performance in small-model text generation.
description: The Amazon Web Services m7a.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring an 8-core AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core, but does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps. Benchmarks highlight strong single-core CPU performance and top-tier text processing capabilities, while multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics remain in the average tier. With support for AVX-512 and cryptographic instructions, the m7a.2xlarge is suited for general-purpose workloads, including web serving, software compilation, text processing, and database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7a.2xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring an 8-core AMD EPYC processor, 32 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 3 Gbps baseline network speed, delivering strong single-core performance.

tagline: A balanced general-purpose server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC cores, high-speed DDR5 memory, and strong single-core processing capabilities.

32.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory at 5 GHz
